  1036	                                              ArrayRef<int> Mask,
  1037	                                              VectorType *Ty, int &Index,
  1038	                                              VectorType *&SubTy) const {
  1039	    if (Mask.empty())
  1040	      return Kind;
  1041	    int NumSrcElts = Ty->getElementCount().getKnownMinValue();
  1042	    // For single-source shuffles, ensure NumSrcElts is at least as large as the maximum index.
  1043	    int MaxIndex = -1;
  1044	    for (int M : Mask) {
  1045	      if (M != -1 && M > MaxIndex)
  1046	        MaxIndex = M;
  1047	    }
  1048	    if (Kind == TTI::SK_PermuteSingleSrc && MaxIndex >= NumSrcElts) {
  1049	      // The mask indices refer to a source larger than the destination.
  1050	      // Adjust NumSrcElts to avoid out-of-bounds assertions.
  1051	      NumSrcElts = MaxIndex + 1;
  1052	    }
  1053	    switch (Kind) {
  1054	    case TTI::SK_PermuteSingleSrc: {
  1055	      if (ShuffleVectorInst::isReverseMask(Mask, NumSrcElts))

  1057	      if (ShuffleVectorInst::isZeroEltSplatMask(Mask, NumSrcElts))

  1061	      if (ShuffleVectorInst::isExtractSubvectorMask(Mask, NumSrcElts, Index) &&
  1062	          (Index + Mask.size()) <= (size_t)NumSrcElts) {
  1063	        SubTy = FixedVectorType::get(Ty->getElementType(), Mask.size());

THOUGHT: Good. However, note that `MaxIndex` could be >= `NumSrcElts * 2` for two-source shuffles, but we only adjust for single-source. That's fine.

Now we need to also consider that `isReverseMask` requires `Mask.size() == NumSrcElts`. With our adjustment, `NumSrcElts` may become larger than `Mask.size()`. For example, in the reproducer: `Mask.size()=4`, `MaxIndex=11`, so `NumSrcElts` becomes 12. Then `isReverseMask` will fail because `Mask.size() != NumSrcElts` (4 != 12). That's fine; it won't classify as reverse. That's acceptable.

But we must also ensure that `isSingleSourceMask` doesn't crash. With `NumSrcElts=12`, the assertion `I < (NumOpElts * 2)` where `NumOpElts` is `NumSrcElts`? Wait, `isSingleSourceMask` is called with `NumSrcElts` as the second argument. In `isReverseMask`, it calls `isSingleSourceMask(Mask, NumSrcElts)`. With `NumSrcElts=12`, the check `I < (NumSrcElts * 2)` becomes `I < 24`. `I=11` passes. So no crash.

Thus this should fix the crash while preserving the ability to improve kind when possible.
